chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-1328 of 2022 ___________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge ___________________________________________________________________ FRESH CASE: 1. For order on CMA No.20150/2023 (Urgent). 2. For order on office objection No.31. 3. For order on CMA No.5966/2022 (Exemption). 4. For order on CMA No.5967/2022 (u/s.151 CPC). 5. For hearing of main case. ----------- Dated; 12th September 2023 Mr. S. Ali Kausar Shah, Advocate for Petitioner. -*-*-*-*-*- 1. Urgency granted. 2. Learned counsel for the petitioner undertakes to comply with office objection before the next date of hearing. 3. Exemption granted subject to all just exceptions. 4&5. Through instant Constitutional Petition, the petitioner has expressed his grievance against inaction on the part of the official respondents, who according to petitioner, are not providing the record of six shops owned by his father Late Ghulam Muhammad in Old Sabzi Mandi, whereas, according to learned counsel for the petitioner, after the death of Ghulam Muhammad, respondent No.8, who was managing the affairs of late Ghulam Muhammad about shops, has fraudulently got alternate shops in New Sabzi Mandi. Per learned counsel, since the official respondents are not providing the record of the subject shops, therefore, petitioner is not in a position to seek appropriate remedy available to him before the competent Court of Law in accordance with law. Let pre-admission notices be issued to the respondents as well as to the Advocate-General Sindh, to be served through first three modes, for a date to be fixed after four weeks, when reply/comments, if any, shall be filed with advance copy to the learned counsel for petitioner.

Per learned counsel, since the official respondents are not providing the record of the subject shops, therefore, petitioner is not in a position to seek appropriate remedy available to him before the competent Court of Law in accordance with law. Let pre-admission notices be issued to the respondents as well as to the Advocate-General Sindh, to be served through first three modes, for a date to be fixed after four weeks, when reply/comments, if any, shall be filed with advance copy to the learned counsel for petitioner. J U D G E J U D G E *Farhan/PS*
